headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Namibia (rotation backfill, never assessed). Note canonical countries.json shows Namibia at rank 49 / composite 50.0 (scanner lists rank 50; close-rank discrepancy — used canonical composite 50.0). Published 50.0 / Functional sustained against compound evidence: (1) 2024 election of Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah as Namibia's first female President — significant gender-leadership signal in Southern Africa; (2) Active Herero/Nama genocide reparations negotiations with Germany — President Nandi-Ndaitwah publicly optimistic April-May 2026 that final agreement can be reached by end of 2026; chiefs' forum and technical task force nearing conclusion of joint-declaration review; (3) Prior Joint Declaration (€1.1B over 30 years) acknowledged genocide but excluded Herero/Nama meaningful participation — sustained domestic-civil-society demand for reparation justice; (4) Land reform: post-apartheid legacy of white-minority land ownership; Namibia high Gini coefficient (among world's highest income inequality); land reform progress mixed; (5) Inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day (May 28 2025) institutionalised — annual sustained memorial framework. Net: positive democratic-consolidation under first female President and active genocide-reparations engagement offset by sustained land-and-economic-inequality and contested reparations-process inclusiveness. Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3, ACT 3, EQU 2.5, BND 3.5, ACC 3, SYS 3, INT 3) consistent with functional-band placement. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 50.0 / Functional; Medium confidence.
rank_discrepancy_note: Scanner lists Namibia at rank 50; canonical countries.json shows rank 49. Composite 50.0 sustained per canonical data.
key_evidence: 2024 election of Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah as Namibia's first female President — democratic-consolidation and gender-leadership signal.

April-May 2026 (in-window): President Nandi-Ndaitwah publicly optimistic that Namibia-Germany genocide reparations final agreement can be reached by end of 2026; chiefs' forum and technical task force review nearing conclusion.

Historical Joint Declaration: Germany acknowledged 1904-1908 Herero (~65,000 deaths, ~80% of population) and Nama (~10,000 deaths, ~50% of population) genocide; pledged €1.1B over 30 years for development in affected regions. BUT excluded meaningful Herero/Nama participation — civil society contests representation.
Germany's position: maintains 'reparations' term not legally applicable to colonial-era crimes; no international legal framework existed at the time — contested by Namibian civil society.
Inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day institutionalised May 28 2025 — annual sustained memorial and reparations-advocacy framework.

Land reform: post-apartheid legacy of white-minority land ownership remains; Namibia among world's highest Gini coefficient; land reform progress mixed; willing-buyer-willing-seller framework retained.
Constitutional democracy operational; multi-party system; press freedom relatively strong (regional comparator); SADC member; UN-system engagement active.
Namibian dollar peg to South African rand and SACU customs union provide regional economic-system scaffolding.

evidence.source: The Namibian — President optimistic on genocide reparations deal by 2026
evidence.finding: Nandi-Ndaitwah public optimism on final reparations agreement by end of 2026; in-window positive INT signal.

evidence.source: The Namibian — Genocide issue nearly finalised — Nandi-Ndaitwah
evidence.finding: Final reparations agreement approaching; chiefs' forum and task force reviewing joint declaration.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Namibia marks inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day with call for reparations
evidence.finding: May 28 2025 inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day institutionalised; annual sustained memorial framework.

evidence.source: ICTJ — Namibia Marks Inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day with Call for Reparations
evidence.finding: Transitional-justice institutional framework documentation; civil society demand for genuine reparations.

evidence.source: CAJ News Africa — Namibia waits for German genocide reparations (January 2026)
evidence.finding: January 2026 status update; sustained reparations-process delays; civil society pressure ongoing.
Post-apartheid land-ownership-asymmetry context; willing-buyer-willing-seller framework; mixed progress.

evidence.source: The Namibian — I cannot say genocide renegotiations are progressing — Nandi-Ndaitwah
evidence.finding: Counter-narrative point: at one stage Nandi-Ndaitwah acknowledged renegotiations not progressing — context for May 2026 optimism.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. Genocide Remembrance Day institutionalisation, active civil society advocacy, and government engagement on historic-injustice detection. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. Reparations-process engagement and Genocide Remembrance Day reflect structural affective-acknowledgment of Herero/Nama suffering; sustained engagement framework. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained. Reparations diplomacy active; land-reform programs operational (willing-buyer-willing-seller); welfare and education programs functional. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. Post-apartheid economic-inequality legacy sustained (very high Gini coefficient); reparations-process Herero/Nama participation contested; land-reform progress mixed. Anchor 2-3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 sustained. Constitutional democracy boundary-of-state-protection clear; SADC regional scaffolding active; reparations-diplomacy respects multilateral boundaries. Anchor 3-4.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. Multi-party democracy and press freedom relatively strong (regional comparator); reparations-process transparency contested. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. SADC, SACU, AU multilateral scaffolding; UN-system engagement; constitutional democracy structurally operational. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Declared reparations-pursuit commitments aligned with operational diplomacy; Nandi-Ndaitwah May 2026 optimism is sustained-engagement signal. Land-reform values-action gap sub-threshold. Anchor 3.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 50.0. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms functional-band placement at lower bound (10 points above critical floor at 40).

negativeSignals: Very high Gini coefficient (among world's highest income inequality); post-apartheid land-ownership asymmetry sustained; Herero/Nama civil society contests reparations-process participation; Germany maintains 'reparations' term not legally applicable; January 2026 reparations-process stagnation noted before May optimism.
positiveSignals: First female President (Nandi-Ndaitwah, elected 2024); active genocide-reparations engagement with optimism for 2026 final agreement; inaugural Genocide Remembrance Day institutionalised May 2025; constitutional democracy operational; multi-party system; press freedom relatively strong; SADC/SACU regional scaffolding; sustained transitional-justice institutional framework.
sector_context: Countries — Southern Africa / SADC cluster. Cross-reference South Africa (Madlanga Commission, recently assessed), Zimbabwe, Botswana, Angola. Namibia distinguished by historic-genocide-reparations diplomacy with Germany and first-female-President leadership. Rotation backfill baseline confirms functional placement. No in-window structural events disrupt baseline.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Namibia at 50.0 / Functional
open_actions_carried: WATCH: Namibia-Germany genocide-reparations final agreement (target end-2026) — material INT/SYS positive trajectory event
Watch: Herero/Nama civil society response to any final agreement (process-inclusion question)
Watch: land-reform progress and any structural-policy change
Watch: 2027 Namibian Genocide Remembrance Day commemoration
Standard rotation re-queue: ~120 days (2026-09-05); accelerate to 60 days if final reparations agreement reached
